source.description: A reviewed six-panel raster contact sheet showing one small right-facing ant carrying one large leaf from pale gesture construction to a finished graphite and colored-pencil sketch.
source.notes: The same three-part ant, exactly six bent legs, right-facing walking pose, leaf angle, and stem-to-mandible connection must persist across all six panels.

process_strategy: Establish the ant's three body beads and the leaf footprint together, build the six-leg walking rhythm beneath that load, connect the stem to the mouth, then add leaf structure, ground marks, and restrained color before the final cleanup.

final_elements.name: three-part ant body and walking axis
final_elements.description: One right-facing head, small thorax, and larger abdomen arranged along a gentle walking gesture.

final_elements.name: large carried leaf footprint and stem route
final_elements.description: One oversized leaf held diagonally above the ant, with a pale stem route aimed toward the mouth.

final_elements.name: exactly six bent legs
final_elements.description: Three readable leg pairs with alternating lifted and planted feet beneath the established body.

final_elements.name: two antennae, eye, and small mandibles
final_elements.description: A tiny visible eye, exactly two curved antennae, and simple mandibles gripping the established stem.

final_elements.name: leaf contour, center vein, side veins, and bite notch
final_elements.description: The carried leaf's clear outer contour, central vein, five restrained side-vein pairs, and one small bite notch.

final_elements.name: ground line, three grass ticks, and contact shadows
final_elements.description: A short broken ground line, exactly three grass ticks, and small graphite shadows beneath the planted feet.

final_elements.name: moss-green leaf and warm-brown ant accents
final_elements.description: Restrained colored-pencil texture that leaves warm paper visible through both the leaf and ant.

frames.step_name: Map the ant and its leaf
frames.visible_job: Places three pale body beads on one right-facing walking axis, then adds the oversized leaf footprint and stem route above them before any region is detailed.
introduces: three-part ant body and walking axis
large carried leaf footprint and stem route

frames.visible_job: Wraps the three body guides with a simple ant silhouette and adds exactly six bent legs in three alternating pairs beneath the load.
requires_prior_elements: three-part ant body and walking axis

frames.step_name: Give the ant a determined face
frames.visible_job: Adds one small eye, exactly two curved antennae, and simple mandibles that visibly grip the existing leaf stem.
introduces: two antennae, eye, and small mandibles
requires_prior_elements: three-part ant body and walking axis
large carried leaf footprint and stem route

frames.step_name: Shape and vein the carried leaf
frames.visible_job: Clarifies the reserved leaf contour, center vein, five restrained side-vein pairs, one small bite notch, and the stem-to-mandible connection.
introduces: leaf contour, center vein, side veins, and bite notch
requires_prior_elements: large carried leaf footprint and stem route
two antennae, eye, and small mandibles

frames.step_name: Ground and color the tiny traveler
frames.visible_job: Adds the broken ground line, exactly three grass ticks, contact shadows, moss-green leaf texture, and warm-brown ant accents to the established drawing.
introduces: ground line, three grass ticks, and contact shadows
moss-green leaf and warm-brown ant accents
requires_prior_elements: three-part ant body and walking axis
large carried leaf footprint and stem route
two antennae, eye, and small mandibles
leaf contour, center vein, side veins, and bite notch

final_step.visible_job: Strengthens keeper contours, deepens the existing moss-green and warm-brown accents, and tidies the established ground shadows without adding new structure.
allowed_changes: deeper moss-green and warm-brown colored-pencil accents on existing forms
restrained graphite shading inside established body and leaf shapes

rejection_checks: The ant must remain one right-facing character with the same three body segments in every panel.
Exactly six legs must be visible after step 2; no leg may appear, disappear, or swap attachment points later.
The same single leaf and stem angle must persist after step 1, and the stem must connect visibly to the mandibles after step 3.
The leaf contour, vein pattern, bite notch, ground marks, and both color families must appear before the finished panel.
No panel may render finished ant detail underneath the carried leaf footprint.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must feel playful and attainable, not like a biology diagram or polished storybook illustration.
